title: Password Hashing, Verification, and Upgrade · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A product-security pair incrementally builds versioned password credential verifier, changes one attacker-controlled input, and keeps earlier Go, accessibility, secret-lifecycle, testing, and evidence invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of versioned password credential verifier while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Store password verifiers with a slow password-hashing construction rather than plaintext, reversible encryption, or fast general hashes.
Select and encode Argon2id memory, time, parallelism, salt, and output parameters from current guidance and measured service budgets.
Choose scrypt or PBKDF2 only from explicit availability and compliance constraints with current calibrated parameters.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Password Hash Not Encrypt
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop cryptographic engineering team handles case 7754 during a post-quantum compatibility canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to admit a versioned password verifier, bound KDF work, and select verify-then-rehash migration; preserve accessible status, non-color meaning, keyboard operation, error recovery, and secret-safe diagnostics; then double attacker-controlled size or work and remain inside the admission budget. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Password Hash Not Encrypt.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for store password verifiers with a slow password-hashing construction rather than plaintext, reversible encryption, or fast general hashes. For Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Password Hash Not Encrypt, keep Crypto Go Password Hash Not Encrypt connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
